item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 from me, I'd actually want to see this guy eat a painting. because it seems both like clickbait and it seems like a joke on those food eating YouTube videos

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow! I hate this guy! +1

For the first entry, I initially thought he was eating one of Monet's water lilies paintings that are a few meters long. It's an even more absurd thing to imagine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The background music gets noticeably louder. It is recognizable as dubstep.

Upvote line.

This is definitely a unique article. To mirror what M_E said above: you took some niche representations of YouTube vlog culture and put them together in a fairly effective manner. There are just so many unorthodox and out-there YouTube trends that happen to reach widely inflated audiences, with sizes unlike anything in the traditional media right now. In the real world, we certainly will never see a near-reality bender that eats paintings for Patreon dollars, but you do a good job capturing the strange aspects of the YouTube scene.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Youtube fast food reviewers is an oddly specific thing to satirize, and making it about art is an odd angle from which to satirize it. And it worked out brilliantly! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was remarkable. CryonicAutumn and I swapped drafts, and I thought, "This deserves a prominent place on the mainlist."

Of course, upvote. Take it, Cryonic. You deserve the +1.

Edit: Three Portlands? Wow. Makes a lot of sense on how BJ would gone to a Location of Interest. One more thing. What would happen if BJ ate a Berryman-Langford Visual Memetic Kill Agent? Or some deadly anart picture?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept at first made me roll my eyes. But by the last addendum I was completely sold. I have some qualms with the Item description (having the videos be unable to be deleted is a bit overkill IMO) but I can't dislike a work that won me over despite such a ridiculous concept. Liked, shared, subscribed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Along with the sheer entertainment value, you sneak in the idea 'if you look at the Mona Lisa and think it's the original, how is that different from actually looking at the original?'

Which is a genuine puzzler. It reminds me of the synthetic diamonds now commercially available, that are 'real' gemstones in every sense except for not having been dug out of the ground.

And I've been exposed to YouTube culture through my sons, so BJ was remarkably plausible.

Definitely a +1, would watch again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That last part really hit too close to home.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta say, I really like articles that deal with internet culture. Have a duly granted +1.

item.title: Credits
item.content:encoded:
Thanks go to GenericSkipper, N_AEpic_Fael, Calamitous1, amazingfantasy, crashington, 301320290, Zyn, and justeago for critique. Additional thanks go to Voct for sending me this over IRC in response to my draft.

+ Deep author commentary
- Close

I have no idea how this popped up in my head. I just remember thinking "A YouTube food reviewer: but they eat priceless masterpieces." Naturally, it spiraled a little out of control and this ended up being written.

The original draft for this was off the walls silly. However, I ended up getting a different image in my head of 4272's character along this time. I ended up actually… kind of respecting his worldview. Like, I don't condone destroying art, but when I was writing how he justifies it to himself, there was an aspect of "this is my life, this is what I do" that I can respect. The second draft ended with a somewhat edgy rant alluding to an aspect of 4272's backstory where he snapped and hurt the kids who bullied him. This didn't end up working well for his character, so I switched it to an accidental, but still angry action brought through poor control of his teleportation. There's some other stuff there to be unpacked, but I don't want to do a death of the author thing and tell-alls kinda ruin the point of having subtle clues.

- Close
